items.headline: Elon Musk says Sam Altman tried to 'steal' charity, as moguls contest $150bn court battle
items.summary: Elon Musk has accused his rival Sam Altman of trying to "steal" a charity as he fights his $150bn (£111bn) court claim over OpenAI.  The trial centres on the 2015 birth of OpenAI, maker of ChatGPT, as a non-profit start-up, primarily funded by Mr Musk, before it evolved into a capitalist venture now valued at $852bn (£632bn).  Mr Musk, the CEO of Tesla and SpaceX, has accused Mr Altman, OpenAI, and its president, Greg Brockman, of wooing his donations by promising to build a non-profit to develop AI responsibly, before pivoting to create a for-profit entity in 2019 to enrich themselves.
